subject: Hernia Emergencies
title: Hernia Traps: Richter's and Femoral
hook: Some hernias kill bowel while looking almost normal. These are the strangulation traps.

points.heading: Richter's: necrosis without obstruction
bullets: Only the antimesenteric wall gets trapped.
The lumen stays patent, so no obstruction.
Bowel can still go ischaemic and perforate.

points.heading: Femoral hernias strangulate
points.icon: a femoral hernia at the groin
bullets: Rigid, narrow femoral ring strangulates more than inguinal.
Inguinal is still the commonest hernia in women.

points.heading: The occult femoral hernia
bullets: Elderly woman, small bowel obstruction, no prior surgery.
Tender groin lump without obstruction? Think Richter's.

points.heading: Never reduce a strangulated hernia
points.icon: a hand crossed out over a lump
bullets: Tender, irreducible, no cough impulse, toxic, febrile.
Reducing it pushes gangrenous bowel back inside.
That bowel can perforate and cause peritonitis.

captionLines: Some hernias kill bowel while looking almost normal.
Richter's stays patent so it necroses without obstruction. The rigid femoral ring strangulates far more often than inguinal.
Suspect an occult femoral hernia in an older woman with bowel obstruction and no surgical history. Never force a strangulated hernia back.
Recall Engine turns any lecture into SBAs, MCQs, flashcards and high-yield notes so these classic strangulation traps become automatic. 🩺
